## Weekly Practice Schedule Structure

To master your instrument effectively, you must dedicate consistent daily time to focused repetition. Allocate at least one hour each day to your training sessions.

## Month 1-3 Skill Acquisition Goals

During the first quarter, your primary focus should be on learning the basic theory, finger dexterity, and playing simple scales. Aim to play through song A successfully without pauses by the end of this period.

## Month 4-6 Intermediate Technique Targets

In the middle of the year, increase tempo significantly and incorporate complex rhythms. Start practicing arpeggios daily and begin recording yourself to compare progress against the initial goals set during Q1.

## Month 7-9 Stage Performance Readiness

Prepare for live application by joining small groups or performing solo at local venues. Maintain rigorous physical conditioning to ensure stamina during longer performances and rehearsals.

## Month 10-12 Final Mastery Checkpoint

Conclude the year with a full recital or submission of a high-difficulty composition. Conduct a self-evaluation covering sound quality, timing, and emotional expression to determine readiness for advanced study.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
## Weekly Practice Schedule Structure

To master your instrument effectively, you must dedicate consistent daily time to focused repetition. Allocate at least one hour each day to your training sessions.

## Month 1-3 Skill Acquisition Goals

During the first quarter, your primary focus should be on learning the basic theory, finger dexterity, and playing simple scales. Aim to play through song A successfully without pauses by the end of this period.

## Month 4-6 Intermediate Technique Targets

In the middle of the year, increase tempo significantly and incorporate complex rhythms. Start practicing arpeggios daily and begin recording yourself to compare progress against the initial goals set during Q1.

## Month 7-9 Stage Performance Readiness

Prepare for live application by joining small groups or performing solo at local venues. Maintain rigorous physical conditioning to ensure stamina during longer performances and rehearsals.

## Month 10-12 Final Mastery Checkpoint

Conclude the year with a full recital or submission of a high-difficulty composition. Conduct a self-evaluation covering sound quality, timing, and emotional expression to determine readiness for advanced study.<|im_end|>
